summary: Large game-mode decompilation PR centered on src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.c, with additional matching work in gm_18A5, gmallstar, gmcamera, gmopening, gmresult, lbbgflash, and sislib. It replaced several /// # stubs with partial or near-matching C implementations for event-mode flow functions, exposed helper prototypes in headers, adjusted library signatures needed by camera text setup, and used small codegen tactics such as noinline wrapper functions, literal float constants, indexed struct access, and loop/switch rewrites. The bot report recorded 3 new matches and 17 unmatched-item improvements, including full matches for gmCamera_801A33BC and gm_801B59AC and an 81.32% result for gm_801BC00C.
what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed gm_1BA8.c functions, including gm_801BAC9C, gm_801BB758, gm_801BBB64, gm_801BC00C, gm_801BCC9C, gm_801BD028, and gm_801BF128.

Updated gm_1BA8.h prototypes for newly implemented or signature-corrected functions, including gm_801BAC9C(GameScene*, s32), gm_801BBB64(void), gm_801BC00C(void), gm_801BC670(HSD_GObj*), and gm_801BF128(void).
Added includes required by the new gm_1BA8.c code paths: sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.h and melee/it/items/itevyoshiegg.h.
Changed gm_801BC670 from void(void) to void(HSD_GObj*) and reduced a PAD_STACK value from 0x18 to 0x10 in the diff excerpt.
In gm_18A5.c, rewrote fn_8018A514 from pointer-increment entry writes to indexed entries[i] writes and changed a BracketSrcEntry pointer lookup to array-style indexing through an offset table.
In gm_18A5.c, replaced several extern sdata2 float labels with immediate literals such as 0.5f, 415.6922f, 149.0f, 537.0f, 47.0f, and 409.0f.

In gmallstar.c, rewrote All-Star data access to use base-relative casts to AllstarRoundInfo and gm_803DEBE8_t data, simplified character-copy logic into loops, and adjusted status/color indexing through byte-level gm_80490940 access.

In gmcamera.c, introduced dont_inline pragmas and explicit noinline wrapper chains around gmCamera_801A25C8 and gmCamera_801A2650, rewrote text cleanup as a for loop, and converted gmCamera_801A33BC control flow from labels/gotos to a switch.
In gmopening.c, implemented gm_801A9DD0 and added static Vec3 constants used to initialize an orthographic camera object, viewport, scissor, eye, interest, near/far, and GX link setup.
In gmresult.c, replaced a manually unrolled four-player team/small-loser check with a for loop and added fn_80174284_noinline as a wrapper used during result entry setup.
In lbbgflash.c, made lbBgFlash_80021A10 non-static and declared it in lbbgflash.h so gm_801BC00C can call lbBgFlash_80021A10(0.2f); also converted several GXColor externs to static definitions.
In sislib.c/h, changed HSD_SisLib_803A6530 from void to u8* and returned the result of HSD_SisLib_803A6478, enabling callers such as gmCamera_801A33BC to use the copied string/table pointer directly.
smart_moves: Used tiny wrapper functions with _noinline suffixes instead of broad pragma blocks where call-shape mattered, e.g. fn_80174284_noinline and the gmCamera/gm_1BA8 wrapper chains.
Improved matching while making control flow more maintainable: gmresult’s four repeated player checks became a single for loop, and gmCamera_801A33BC’s label/goto branch tree became a switch.

Corrected a library helper signature rather than casting around it locally: HSD_SisLib_803A6530 now returns the u8* produced by HSD_SisLib_803A6478, which matches downstream usage in camera text construction.
Promoted lbBgFlash_80021A10 to a public header declaration only when a game-mode implementation needed to call it, keeping the change minimal and evidence-linked.

Preserved address-style placeholder naming for unknown game-mode symbols while still adding concrete prototypes and headers, allowing other files to call the new work without overnaming uncertain behavior.
Moved some rodata/sdata2 knowledge into C literals or static constants where the diff showed exact values, reducing reliance on orphan extern labels.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib routine’s return value is used by an original caller, fixing the shared prototype can unlock cleaner matching in game-mode code; HSD_SisLib_803A6530 returning u8* is the example here.
For decomp codegen, noinline call-shape can be localized with wrapper functions instead of keeping larger functions under pragma dont_inline blocks.
Do not assume pointer-increment form is always better for matching; fn_8018A514 improved by using entries[i] indexed stores and an indexed offset-table lookup.
If asset or table data is laid out relative to a loaded base pointer, base-plus-offset casts may match better than direct global-symbol indexing, as shown in gmallstar.c.

Small sdata2 changes can have mixed results: the bot reported +3 bytes for gm_1BA8 .sdata2 but a -4 byte regression for gmopening .sdata2, so literal/static-const moves should be checked against data matching.

Event-mode implementations often need cross-system prototypes, including background flash, item spawning, camera setup, scene/proc setup, player state, and DVD preload/cache functions; header hygiene matters when stubs become real code.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-named game-mode functions kept address-style names such as gm_801BAC9C, gm_801BB758, gm_801BBB64, gm_801BC00C, gm_801BCC9C, gm_801BD028, and gm_801BF128.
Wrapper helpers used explicit suffixes like _noinline, and in gm_1BA8/gmcamera some wrappers used layered suffixes such as _inner, _inner2, and _inner3 to force call/inlining shape.
Header prototypes retained address comments such as /* 1BAC9C */ and /* 021A10 */.
Uncertain struct fields remained offset-style names such as xB_1, xB_2, x20, x44, x4C, x50, unk_530, and unk_535 rather than being prematurely semanticized.
Static data labels retained decomp label names such as gm_803B7D68, gm_803B7D74, lbl_804D3840, lbl_804D3844, lbl_804D3848, and lbl_804D384C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Localized noinline behavior through wrapper calls: gmCamera_801A25C8_noinline, gmCamera_801A2650_noinline/noinline2/noinline3, fn_80174284_noinline, gm_801BEFA4_noinline, and gm_801BEFC0_noinline.
Replaced extern float labels with immediate constants in gm_18A5.c to influence sdata2/codegen, including 0.5f and camera/table layout constants.
Used indexed array stores in fn_8018A514 instead of advancing a BracketEntry* variable.
Changed signedness and local types where needed, such as u32 to s32 in gmCamera_801A253C locals and s32 i to int i in fn_8018A514.
Adjusted explicit stack padding in gm_801BC670 from PAD_STACK(0x18) to PAD_STACK(0x10).
Used base-relative pointer arithmetic and casts in gmallstar.c to access packed All-Star round/opponent tables.
Changed HSD_SisLib_803A6530 to return the copied u8* so gmCamera_801A2224 could receive HSD_SisLib_803A6530(3, 0x15, 0x11) directly.
Converted goto-heavy or unrolled code into switch/for forms where the diff showed improved matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 68.49% (+572 bytes) and matched data to 39.77% (+24 bytes).
The bot reported 3 new matches: gmCamera_801A33BC (+221 bytes to 100%), gm_801B59AC (+69 bytes to 100%), and gmopening .rodata (+24 bytes to 100%).
The bot reported 17 improvements in unmatched items, notably gm_801BC00C to 81.32%, gm_801BB758 to 94.28%, gm_801BF128 to 89.67%, gm_801BCC9C to 95.05%, and gm_801BBB64 to 72.02%.
The bot reported one unmatched-item regression: gmopening .sdata2 dropped by 4 bytes, from 50.00% to 40.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching gm_801BC00C, gm_801BBB64, gm_801BB758, gm_801BCC9C, gm_801BD028, or gm_801BF128.
Search for later renames or struct refinements around struct EventData, gm_804D6900_t, gm_804D6900_x4_t, and AllstarRoundInfo.
Check whether the gmopening .sdata2 regression from this PR was later recovered.
Search for other PRs that changed HSD_SisLib_803A6530 callers after its return type became u8*.
Look for subsequent cleanup replacing wrapper-chain noinline tactics with cleaner matching once surrounding functions were decompiled.
